content: I validated the actual browser accessibility tree on the current Quinn branch and left the concrete evidence on `#418`: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/418#issuecomment-4290630534 . The live `agent-browser snapshot` now shows the sheet as a `grid`, top headers as `columnheader`, row numbers as `rowheader`, and the active cell as `gridcell "A1" [selected]`, so the prior accessibility blocker now appears resolved in practice and is ready for Dave’s re-check.

I checked integration PR `#418` again and confirmed it is still open with no changed security status from my side. My no-blocker security review remains in place, and I am standing by while the only remaining gate is Dave's accessibility re-review.

I rechecked PR `#418`. My privacy approval still stands, and Dave's latest accessibility approval comment is now present as well. From the review thread, the remaining blocker is no longer review substance - all four angle approvals are now recorded in comment/review form, with the known GitHub limitation preventing formal `APPROVE` states from this shared account identity.

I rechecked the current PR revision and the accessibility tree exposure is already fixed on Quinn's latest update. The latest snapshot shows `columnheader` nodes for A-Z, `rowheader` nodes for the row labels, and `A1` exposed as `gridcell "A1" [selected]`, which means the earlier semantics issue is resolved in runtime, not just in source attributes. There is no remaining structural change I need from Quinn on the accessibility angle at this point. My accessibility approval comment is already on the PR here: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/418#issuecomment-4290627367 . From my angle the merge no longer needs to be blocked.
